raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact makes an explicit contribution claim ('novel approach') and a gap statement (geo-distributed partitions are the motivating challenge); any meaningful review must assess whether these are clearly and accurately articulated.","R1.2":"The section contains logical claims requiring consistency checking: 'eventual consistency in the absence of partitions' paired with 'bounded staleness during partitions'; the staleness formula max(2T, 50ms); and cross-section coherence with Sections 3, 4, and 5 referenced but not present in the artifact.","R1.3":"Specific quantitative claims are asserted (p99 8.4ms within-region, 142ms cross-region, 50k writes/sec sustained load, 47-minute partition case study with zero inconsistencies) without in-section evidence tables or figures; verifying plausibility and internal consistency of these numbers is central to reviewing this artifact.","R1.6":"Paragraph 1 contains a redundancy ('challenge' appears in consecutive sentences); the section transitions abruptly from protocol description to experimental results to a formal bound with no bridging narrative; prose polish is a standard component of a section review."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize technical correctness (logical consistency of the protocol guarantees and alignment of experimental claims with stated methodology) or writing quality (prose clarity and narrative structure), or are both equally load-bearing?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes an explicit contribution claim ('novel approach') and a gap statement (geo-distributed partitions are the motivating challenge); any meaningful review must assess whether these are clearly and accurately articulated.
rationale.R1.2: The section contains logical claims requiring consistency checking: 'eventual consistency in the absence of partitions' paired with 'bounded staleness during partitions'; the staleness formula max(2T, 50ms); and cross-section coherence with Sections 3, 4, and 5 referenced but not present in the artifact.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted (p99 8.4ms within-region, 142ms cross-region, 50k writes/sec sustained load, 47-minute partition case study with zero inconsistencies) without in-section evidence tables or figures; verifying plausibility and internal consistency of these numbers is central to reviewing this artifact.
rationale.R1.6: Paragraph 1 contains a redundancy ('challenge' appears in consecutive sentences); the section transitions abruptly from protocol description to experimental results to a formal bound with no bridging narrative; prose polish is a standard component of a section review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical correctness (logical consistency of the protocol guarantees and alignment of experimental claims with stated methodology) or writing quality (prose clarity and narrative structure), or are both equally load-bearing?
